hero_description: Master the fundamentals of Electric Vehicles and launch your career in India's fastest-growing automotive sector. This hands-on certification program equips you with essential knowledge about EV technology, battery systems, charging infrastructure, and maintenance—preparing you for immediate employment in the rapidly expanding EV industry across India.
overview_description: This comprehensive 8-week foundation course covers everything you need to know about Electric Vehicles, from basic principles of electric propulsion to practical troubleshooting and safety protocols. You'll learn about battery technology, charging systems, electrical components, and gain practical experience with real EV systems used in Indian markets. Perfect for school leavers, career-changers, and aspiring technicians looking to enter the booming EV sector.

modules.title: Introduction to Electric Vehicles and Industry Overview
topics: History and evolution of Electric Vehicles globally and in India — from early concepts to modern Tesla, Tata Nexon EV, Mahindra XUV400, and local brands
Types of Electric Vehicles — Battery Electric Vehicles (BEV), Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicles (PHEV), Hybrid Electric Vehicles (HEV), and Fuel Cell Vehicles (FCV)
Advantages and disadvantages of EVs — cost savings, environmental benefits, performance, range anxiety, and infrastructure challenges in India
Indian EV market landscape — government policies, FAME India scheme, subsidies, and growth projections for 2024-2030
Comparison between traditional ICE (Internal Combustion Engine) vehicles and Electric Vehicles — efficiency, emissions, operating costs
Global EV manufacturers vs Indian EV manufacturers — understanding market leaders like Tata, Mahindra, BYD, MG Motor, and emerging startups
Career opportunities in EV sector — technician, service engineer, charging station operator, battery specialist, and entrepreneurship options
Environmental impact and sustainability — carbon footprint reduction, recycling, and circular economy in EV industry
Government initiatives and incentives — FAME-II scheme, state subsidies, GST benefits, and charging infrastructure development programs

modules.title: Electrical Fundamentals and EV Power Systems
topics: Basic electrical concepts — voltage, current, resistance, power, and Ohm's Law with practical EV applications
AC (Alternating Current) vs DC (Direct Current) — understanding three-phase AC, single-phase AC, and DC systems used in EVs
Electrical circuits in EVs — series circuits, parallel circuits, and mixed circuits with real-world EV examples
Safety in electrical systems — earthing, grounding, circuit protection, fuses, circuit breakers, and MCBs used in EV charging
Power distribution systems in EVs — high-voltage systems (400V+), low-voltage systems (12V), and isolation between them
Electrical measuring instruments — multimeter, clamp meter, insulation tester, and how to use them safely on EV systems
Common electrical faults in EVs — short circuits, open circuits, ground faults, and diagnostic procedures
Wiring and cable standards for EVs — IEC standards, Indian standards, color coding, and cable sizing calculations
Protection systems — relays, contactors, solenoids, and their role in EV safety and control
Practical hands-on — measuring voltage and current in EV charging systems, testing circuits, and safe handling of tools

modules.title: Battery Technology and Energy Storage Systems
topics: Battery fundamentals — how batteries work, electrochemistry basics, and energy conversion principles
Lithium-ion battery technology — cell structure, chemistry, advantages over lead-acid, and why it's standard in modern EVs
Battery specifications and parameters — voltage, capacity (Ah/kWh), energy density, power density, and C-rating explained
Battery pack architecture — series and parallel cell arrangements, cell balancing, and thermal management in EV batteries
Battery Management System (BMS) — monitoring, balancing, temperature control, voltage regulation, and safety functions
State of Charge (SOC) and State of Health (SOH) — calculation methods, importance, and how BMS maintains battery health
Temperature management in batteries — cooling systems, thermal sensors, and why overheating reduces battery life
Battery degradation and cycle life — understanding capacity fade, calendar aging, and extending battery lifespan through proper charging
Alternative battery technologies — solid-state batteries, LFP (Lithium Iron Phosphate), and emerging technologies for future EVs
Battery safety and hazards — thermal runaway, overcharging, deep discharge, and emergency procedures
Battery recycling and second-life applications — environmental responsibility, circular economy, and emerging Indian recycling startups
Practical hands-on — disassembling battery modules, identifying cell types, measuring battery voltage, and understanding BMS display readings

modules.title: EV Charging Infrastructure and Systems
topics: Charging standards in India — Type 2 connectors, CCS (Combined Charging System), CHAdeMO, and Indian standards for AC/DC charging
Charging levels explained — Level 1 (household 230V), Level 2 (wall-mounted 32A), DC fast charging (50-350 kW), and charging time comparisons
AC charging systems — onboard chargers, power electronics, and how vehicles convert AC to DC for battery charging
DC fast charging systems — charging station components, power conversion, cooling systems, and grid integration challenges
Home charging setup — installing 7.4 kW and 11 kW chargers, electrical requirements, safety considerations, and cost in India
Public charging networks in India — NITI Aayog charging stations, private networks (Fortum, Shell, Tata Power), and coverage across states
Charging station operation and maintenance — monitoring systems, payment methods, troubleshooting common issues, and user support
Grid integration and smart charging — load management, vehicle-to-grid (V2G) technology, and demand response programs
Battery charging optimization — fast charging vs slow charging, optimal charging strategies, and impact on battery longevity
Safety protocols during charging — PPE requirements, electrical safety, emergency procedures, and customer safety guidelines
Practical hands-on — connecting EV to different charger types, reading charging display data, identifying charger faults, and safe disconnection procedures

modules.title: EV Components, Systems, and Vehicle Architecture
topics: Electric motor types — AC induction motors, permanent magnet motors, and switched reluctance motors used in Indian EVs
Motor specifications — power output (kW), torque (Nm), RPM range, efficiency, and thermal characteristics
Motor control systems — inverters, motor controllers, and how they manage speed and torque delivery
Power transmission in EVs — single-speed reduction gearbox, direct drive, and advantages over multi-gear transmissions
Regenerative braking system — how EVs recover energy during braking, efficiency gains, and impact on range
Braking systems in EVs — friction brakes, brake-by-wire technology, ABS integration, and safety considerations
Steering and suspension — modifications for EV weight distribution, lower center of gravity, and handling characteristics
HVAC systems in EVs — cabin heating (heat pump technology), cooling, defogging, and energy consumption management
Vehicle Control Unit (VCU) and onboard electronics — central processing, sensor integration, and communication protocols (CAN bus)
Safety systems — airbags, stability control, collision detection, and EV-specific safety features
Auxiliary systems — 12V power distribution, lighting systems, windshield wipers, and low-voltage power management
Practical hands-on — identifying major EV components, understanding component layouts, reading system diagrams, and tracing electrical connections

modules.title: EV Maintenance, Troubleshooting, and Career Development
topics: Preventive maintenance in EVs — service intervals, fluid checks (coolant, brake fluid), filter replacements, and tire maintenance
Battery health monitoring — SOH assessment, capacity testing, cell voltage measurement, and when to recommend battery service
Troubleshooting electrical faults — using diagnostic tools, reading error codes, interpreting BMS messages, and systematic fault-finding
Common EV problems and solutions — range degradation, slow charging, battery imbalance, motor noise, and brake issues
Software updates and diagnostics — OBD-II readers for EVs, manufacturer diagnostic tools, and remote diagnostics via cloud platforms
Safety protocols during EV service — high-voltage safety, lockout-tagout procedures, insulation testing, and emergency disconnection
Tire and wheel maintenance — specific tire requirements for EVs, pressure monitoring, and impact on range
Cabin climate control troubleshooting — heat pump diagnostics, refrigerant handling, and energy efficiency optimization
Brake system maintenance — brake fluid flushing, pad replacement, and regenerative braking system calibration
Electrical connector maintenance — corrosion prevention, contact cleaning, and waterproofing for charging connectors
Career pathways in EV sector — EV technician, service advisor, charging station operator, battery specialist, and entrepreneurship opportunities
Skill development and certifications — advanced EV courses, manufacturer training programs, and international certifications (IMR, IMERC)
Entrepreneurship in EV sector — starting a charging station business, mobile repair service, battery reconditioning, and franchising opportunities
Practical hands-on — complete vehicle inspection checklist, battery health assessment, charging system diagnostics, fault code interpretation, and customer communication skills

assessment.theory_format: 25 Questions × 2 Marks each (Multiple Choice + Short Answer)
assessment.practical_format: Hands-on assessment of EV systems, charging procedures, diagnostics, and safety protocols

practical_items.title: EV Component Identification and System Understanding
practical_items.description: Candidate must identify major EV components (motor, battery pack, inverter, BMS, charger), explain their functions, and trace electrical connections on an actual EV or training model. Submit detailed component diagram with labels and functional descriptions.

practical_items.title: Battery and Charging System Diagnostics
practical_items.description: Candidate must perform battery health check using BMS data, measure voltage across battery modules, test charging system output, and identify any faults. Submit diagnostic report with readings, analysis, and recommendations.

practical_items.title: Safe Charging Procedure and Troubleshooting
practical_items.description: Candidate must safely connect an EV to AC and DC chargers, monitor charging process, interpret charging display data, and troubleshoot common charging issues. Demonstrate proper safety protocols and customer communication.

practical_items.title: Electrical Safety and Preventive Maintenance
practical_items.description: Candidate must demonstrate high-voltage safety procedures, perform insulation testing, check coolant levels, inspect brake system, and create a maintenance checklist. Submit safety checklist and maintenance report.

eligibility: Minimum age: 14 years (with parental consent for candidates below 18)
Minimum education: Class 8th pass or equivalent
No prior experience needed — completely beginner-friendly
Already working in automotive sector? Apply for RPL (Recognition of Prior Learning) exam
Valid government ID proof required at registration (Aadhaar, PAN, Voter ID, Driving License)
Medical fitness certificate may be required for practical training involving high-voltage systems
Open to all candidates regardless of gender, caste, religion, or economic background
Recommended: Basic computer literacy for understanding digital diagnostic tools
learning_outcomes: Understand the fundamentals of Electric Vehicle technology, architecture, and how they differ from traditional vehicles
Identify and explain major EV components including motors, batteries, inverters, and charging systems
Perform safe electrical measurements and diagnostics on EV systems using appropriate tools and safety protocols
Operate different types of EV chargers (AC Level 2, DC fast charging) and troubleshoot common charging issues
Assess battery health, understand State of Charge and State of Health, and perform basic battery diagnostics
Execute preventive maintenance procedures and systematic troubleshooting of electrical and mechanical EV faults
Apply high-voltage safety protocols and emergency procedures when working on EV systems
Identify career opportunities in the EV sector and plan professional development in this rapidly growing industry

career_paths.title: EV Service Technician
career_paths.description: Work at authorized EV service centers, dealerships, or multi-brand workshops. Diagnose, maintain, and repair electric vehicles. High demand across major cities like Bangalore, Mumbai, Delhi, and Pune.

career_paths.title: EV Charging Station Operator
career_paths.description: Operate and maintain public/private EV charging stations. Ensure smooth customer experience, monitor equipment, and perform regular maintenance. Growing opportunities with NITI Aayog and private networks.

career_paths.title: Battery Specialist/Technician
career_paths.description: Specialize in battery diagnostics, health monitoring, and replacement. Work with battery manufacturers or service centers. Highest growth potential as battery technology becomes critical.

career_paths.title: EV Charging Station Entrepreneur
career_paths.description: Start your own charging station business in residential societies, commercial complexes, or highways. Government subsidies available under FAME-II scheme. Initial investment: ₹5-15 lakhs depending on location and charger type.

career_paths.title: Mobile EV Repair Service Owner
career_paths.description: Provide on-site EV repair and maintenance services. Visit customer locations for diagnostics and repairs. Growing demand in metro cities where convenience is valued.

career_paths.title: EV Service Advisor/Sales
career_paths.description: Work at EV dealerships as service advisor or sales consultant. Explain EV technology to customers, manage service bookings, and handle customer relationships. Excellent communication skills required.

career_paths.title: EV Training Instructor
career_paths.description: After gaining 2-3 years of experience, become a trainer at vocational institutes, manufacturer training centers, or corporate training programs. Teach next generation of EV technicians.

career_paths.title: Battery Recycling Technician
career_paths.description: Work with battery recycling startups and facilities. Extract valuable materials from used EV batteries. Emerging sector with high growth potential and environmental impact.

course_highlights: 100% practical hands-on training with real EV systems
Access to latest EV diagnostic tools and equipment
Training on vehicles like Tata Nexon EV, Mahindra XUV400, MG ZS EV, and other popular Indian EVs
Guidance on government subsidies and FAME-II scheme benefits
Lifetime job placement support and alumni network
Flexible batch timings (morning, evening, weekend options)
Digital learning materials and video tutorials for self-study
training_methodology: Classroom lectures with real-world case studies
Hands-on practical training in equipped workshop
Live demonstrations on actual EV systems
post_course_support: Job placement assistance with top EV service centers
Advanced course recommendations (EV Advanced Diagnostics, Battery Management Systems)
Alumni network for peer learning and job referrals
Continuous skill updates through webinars and workshops
